context: Tests whether the agent writes commit messages in imperative mood, includes explanatory body text for complex non-obvious changes, and references the related issue numbers. All three change sets involve bug fixes with clear issue tracker tickets and non-trivial root causes that warrant explanation.

checklist.name: Imperative mood — commit 1
checklist.description: The subject line of Commit 1 uses imperative mood (e.g., 'handle', 'fix', 'guard') — NOT past tense ('fixed', 'handled', 'added')

checklist.name: Imperative mood — commit 2
checklist.description: The subject line of Commit 2 uses imperative mood — NOT past tense

checklist.name: Imperative mood — commit 3
checklist.description: The subject line of Commit 3 uses imperative mood — NOT past tense

checklist.name: Body present — commit 1
checklist.description: Commit 1 includes a multi-line body (not just the subject line) explaining why the change was made

checklist.name: Body present — commit 2
checklist.description: Commit 2 includes a multi-line body explaining why the change was made

checklist.name: Body present — commit 3
checklist.description: Commit 3 includes a multi-line body explaining why the change was made

checklist.name: fix type used
checklist.description: All three commits use the `fix` type prefix (not `patch`, `hotfix`, or other invented types)
